info.description: Five-class mini_data_v2 static annotations for M2 Transformer

annotations.caption: Small, pale spots with yellow halos on the leaf surface.
Small, irregular spots with yellow halos and dark centers spread across the leaf surface.
Center of spots is dark, necrotic, with tissue decay or holes.
Anthracnose spots start small, irregular, then expand into dark, circular lesions on leaves.
Leaf has tan, irregular lesions with holes, characteristic of anthracnose.
Small, water-soaked spots on leaves, gray with yellow halos, some with cracks.
Irregular, sunken lesions with dark margins and gray centers; some spots have black specks on leaf.
Center of spots is grayish, sunken, and cracked.
Irregular, angular spots expand, merge; dark edges, gray centers.
Image shows leaf with anthracnose, not fruit infection.
Small, light brown lesions with faint yellow halos on the leaf.
Multiple dark lesions with necrotic spots and small holes scattered across the leaf.
Center of spots shows grayish-brown necrotic tissue with possible fungal growth.
Circular dark spots expand, often merging into larger lesions on leaves.
Leaf shows irregular brown lesions with gray centers and small holes, typical of anthracnose.
Small, water-soaked spots turning brown with yellow halos on leaf surface.
Multiple irregular brown lesions with yellow halos and small spots on the leaf.
Center of spots is dark brown, necrotic, with dry, cracked texture.
Circular spots with yellow margins, expanding to irregular shapes.
Leaf shows brown, irregular lesions with yellow halos, typical of anthracnose.
Small, water-soaked spots develop, turning brown with irregular edges and small holes.
Multiple dark lesions with holes indicate anthracnose spread and tissue decay on the leaf.
Center darkens, becomes necrotic, and may crack with age.
Anthracnose spots start small, dark, then expand into irregular, sunken lesions on cucumber leaves.
Leaf shows brown lesions, spots, and holes from anthracnose infection.
Small dark circular spots on leaf surface, some with necrotic centers.
Lesions, holes, and dark spots on leaf indicate anthracnose spread with necrotic tissue.
Centers show dark, necrotic, blackened tissue.
Small dark circular spots with target rings; expand to irregular shapes.
Leaf shows dark, sunken lesions, holes, and brown discoloration from anthracnose infection.

Centers are dark, necrotic, and sunken.
Dark, circular/irregular spots expand, with yellow margins on cucumber leaves.
Image shows leaf, not fruit. Anthracnose on fruit has sunken, dark lesions.
Small dark spots with a black center and irregular yellowing on the leaf.
Dark lesions with yellow halos, cracked edges, and tissue decay spreading on leaf.

Circular spots with dark centers, light brown margins, expanding and merging.

Circular, brown spots that expand, sometimes merging on cucumber leaves.

Initial symptom: sudden daytime wilting, partial night recovery, then irreversible decline.
Bacterial Wilt causes cucumber leaf margins to yellow, then rapidly wilt and turn brown.
Stem cankers are dark, sunken lesions. Sticky bacterial ooze is clear, glistening fluid from infected stems.
Yellow spots turn brown, forming shot-hole lesions as Bacterial Wilt spreads on cucumber leaves.
Bacterial Wilt causes cucumber plants to wilt fully, stem base browns, leaves die rapidly. Complete death occurs.
Daytime wilting, nighttime recovery; leaves dry and brown in later stages.
Bacterial Wilt causes cucumber leaf margins to yellow, progressing inward, with wilting and browning.

Yellow spots turn brown, forming shot-hole lesions as Bacterial Wilt spreads on cucumber leaves.
Bacterial Wilt causes complete wilting, browning at stem base, and death of cucumber plant as xylem clogs, leading to desiccation.
Daytime wilting with nighttime recovery is the initial symptom before permanent damage.
